services.description: Wood-destroying insects cause silent, costly damage. A dedicated WDI inspection identifies active infestations and prior damage that standard inspections may not uncover.

sections.heading: Home Inspection Services in Massachusetts
sections.intro: Ken Arnold Home Inspection LLC delivers thorough, independent residential property inspections across the Pioneer Valley, Connecticut River Valley, and surrounding communities. With 22 years of licensed experience and comprehensive digital reports, you get the clarity you need to make confident decisions.

sections.heading: Every Property Tells a Story
sections.subheading: A Licensed Home Inspector Reads It

paragraphs.html: Whether you're purchasing your first home in Amherst, preparing to list a property in Northampton, or evaluating a multi-family investment in Greenfield, a professional home inspection is your single best tool for understanding what you're working with. It's not about passing or failing — it's about knowing.

As an independent home inspector in Massachusetts (License #587), Ken Arnold works for you — not the seller, not the agent, and not the lender. That independence means your inspection report reflects exactly what was found, with annotated photos, plain-language explanations, and actionable recommendations. After 22 years of inspecting homes throughout the Pioneer Valley, there's very little that surprises Ken — and nothing that gets overlooked.

sections.heading: From Scheduling
sections.intro: A home inspection with Ken Arnold in Pioneer Valley follows a straightforward, reliable process. Everything is planned, with no surprises or delays. Here is exactly what you can expect.

cards.title: Schedule & Confirm
cards.body: Call or book online. Ken personally handles every appointment — no dispatchers, no subcontractors. You'll get a confirmed date, a clear price, and a direct phone number for any questions before inspection day.

cards.title: On-Site Inspection Day
cards.body: Ken inspects the property systematically — roof to foundation, structure to systems. You're welcome to walk along and ask questions. A typical residential property inspection takes 2–3 hours depending on size and condition.

cards.title: Detailed Findings Review
cards.body: After the walkthrough, Ken reviews key findings with you on site — major concerns, maintenance items, and safety issues. You leave the inspection knowing the property's true condition before the written report even arrives.

cards.title: Detailed Digital Report
cards.body: Your comprehensive inspection report — complete with annotated photos, clear descriptions, and actionable recommendations — is delivered digitally and organized by safety priority, so you know exactly what you're working with.

sections.heading: Full-Scope Inspection Services
sections.intro: Every property and every client has different needs. Ken Arnold Home Inspection LLC offers a full range of residential and specialized inspection services across Massachusetts — all performed by one experienced, licensed inspector who knows the region's housing stock inside out.

paragraphs.html: Large inspection companies rotate employees through your appointment. Ken Arnold personally conducts every inspection — the same licensed professional who answers your call is the one on your roof, in your crawl space, and writing your report. That consistency is rare, and it matters.

checklist.text: Fully independent — no franchise, no conflicts of interest
Comprehensive digital inspection report with annotated photos
Clients welcome on-site during the entire inspection
Deep knowledge of New England housing stock

sections.heading: What Homeowners and Agents Say
sections.intro: Real feedback from buyers, sellers, and real estate professionals who've trusted Ken with their inspections.

items.quote: "Ken was incredibly thorough and patient with our questions. As first-time buyers, we had no idea what to look for — his report made everything clear and gave us real confidence in our purchase."
items.name: Sarah M.

items.quote: "I've worked with Ken on dozens of transactions over the years. His reports are consistently detailed, his communication with my clients is professional, and turnaround is fast. Highly recommend."
items.name: David R.

items.quote: "We used Ken for a pre-listing inspection and it saved our sale. He found a foundation issue we didn't know about — we fixed it before listing and avoided a $12,000 renegotiation. Worth every penny."
items.name: Mark & Julie T.

items.q: How long does a typical home inspection in Massachusetts take?
items.a: Most residential property inspections take between 2 and 3 hours, depending on the home's size, age, and condition. Larger homes, older properties, and multi-family buildings may require additional time. Ken never rushes an inspection — thoroughness is the priority.

items.q: What does the home inspection report include?
items.a: Your digital report covers every major system and component — structure, roof, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, insulation, windows, doors, and more. Each finding includes annotated photos, a clear description, and a recommendation organized by safety priority.

items.q: Can I attend the inspection in person?
items.a: Absolutely — and Ken encourages it. Walking through the property together lets you see conditions firsthand, ask questions, and understand the home's maintenance needs. It's one of the most valuable parts of the inspection experience.

items.q: Do you offer radon testing and mold inspections alongside the standard inspection?
items.a: Yes. Radon testing, mold inspections, and other specialized services can be added to any standard home inspection. Bundling services saves time and ensures nothing falls through the cracks.

items.q: What areas of Massachusetts do you serve?
items.a: Ken Arnold Home Inspection LLC is based in Deerfield and serves the Pioneer Valley, Connecticut River Valley, and surrounding communities — including Amherst, Northampton, Greenfield, Hadley, Easthampton, Holyoke, Chicopee, and many more. Visit our service area pages for full details.

items.q: How quickly will I receive my inspection report?
items.a: Ken delivers your photo-documented digital report promptly after the inspection — always well inside your due-diligence window. The report is organized by safety priority, with annotated photos and clear recommendations for every finding.

items.q: Is Ken Arnold an independent inspector, or part of a larger company?
items.a: Ken is fully independent. He's not affiliated with any real estate agency, repair company, or franchise. That independence means your report has zero conflicts of interest — the findings reflect only what was observed, without outside pressure. The Massachusetts Board of Registration of Home Inspectors oversees all licensed inspectors in the state.

items.q: What does a home inspection cost in Massachusetts?
items.a: Pricing depends on the property's size, age, and the services requested. Ken provides clear, upfront pricing with no hidden fees. Visit the pricing page for current rates, or schedule online to book your inspection.

sections.heading: Ready to Schedule
sections.body: Whether you're buying, selling, or protecting your investment, Ken Arnold delivers the thorough, independent home inspection services Massachusetts homeowners trust. Call today or schedule online — comprehensive digital reports and no conflicts of interest.
